Patch Set 1:

Liang, are the cfs_cpt_* functions intended to be used in other modules, or are they internal functions?

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

yes, cfs_cpt_* could be used by other modules in the future, but I think cfs_cpu_* and cfs_node_* can be internal functions.

Patch Set 1:

If they are not used, they shouldn't be exported. A future module can re-insert the exports if needed.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

> If they are not used, they shouldn't be exported. A future module can re-insert the exports if needed.

I think it's not worth the churn this will create. Sorry.

Patch Set 1:

I remoove cfs_cpt_* from the patch but left cfs_cpu_* and cfs_node_*.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(16 comments)

It looks like this is just exposing the tip of an iceberg w.r.t. removal of dead code.  Just removing the EXPORT_SYMBOL() will hide that code I think, so better to remove some of this while it is in our sight.

When removing dead functions, please also be sure to remove the function prototypes from the headers (git grep is useful in this regard).

parents.subject: LU-6800 obdclass: change spinlock of key to rwlock

commit.subject: LU-5829 libcfs: remove unnecessary EXPORT_SYMBOL
commit.message:
LU-5829 libcfs: remove unnecessary EXPORT_SYMBOL

A lot of symbols don't need to be exported at all because they are
only used in the module they belong to.

Signed-off-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
Change-Id: Ie386a75bb8d9b5b77360ea2c714aba886dd8f780
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13319
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
